programs.name: HUSKY Health (Medicaid for seniors/disabled)
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage including long-term care services for eligible low-income seniors and disabled individuals. In Connecticut, it is administered through the Department of Social Services as HUSKY Health plans.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and adults with disabilities meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescription drugs, and nursing home care.

programs.name: Connecticut Home Care Program for Elders (CHCPE) - Medicare Savings
programs.what_it_does: Covers Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing (QMB, SLMB, QI) for eligible low-income seniors. Administered via CHCPE financial eligibility screening.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income thresholds for QMB, SLMB, QI.
programs.benefit_value: Pays Medicare Part A/B premiums (up to $170+/month for SLMB) and co-pays.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (CT PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Administered through approved CT providers as part of federal PACE program.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors 55+ who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care including medical, therapies, transportation, and meals.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food at approved retailers. Administered by CT Department of Social Services.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ or households meeting income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+ per month per eligible senior household.

programs.name: Connecticut Energy Assistance Program (CEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating costs during winter months. Administered by Department of Social Services.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors meeting income eligibility.
programs.benefit_value: Grants from $200-$1,500+ based on household size and income.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and furnace repairs. Administered through CT community action agencies.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free weatherization services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.

programs.name: CHOICES (CT SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free Medicare counseling, education, and assistance on benefits and options. Administered by CT Department on Aging.[2][4]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries, families, and caregivers in Connecticut.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save thousands on premiums/coverage.

programs.name: Connecticut Statewide Respite Care Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite services to relieve family caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's or related dementias. Offers in-home and out-of-home care up to 30 days/year.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's/dementia.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $3,500/year and 30 days respite services.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training for low-income seniors. Administered through CT Area Agencies on Aging.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work experience (20 hrs/week) leading to employment.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services to low-income seniors on issues like housing, benefits, and consumer protection. Funded through Area Agencies on Aging.[2][6]

programs.name: Long Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints and protecting rights. Administered statewide by Department on Aging.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other LT facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; resolves 80%+ of complaints.
